_meta.description: Classical Chinese dictionary for reader hover-parsing
_meta.key_format: The character itself e.g. 人 not ren

學.notes: Opening character of the Analects. Traditional form shows hands above a child under a roof.

而.notes: Connects clauses: additive, adversative, or sequential. Never acts as a noun.

時.eng: time, at the right time, seasonally
時.notes: As adverb: at due times — practising at the appropriate moment.

習.eng: to practise, to review, to repeat
習.notes: Traditional form shows a bird's wings above the sun — a young bird repeatedly practising flight.

之.eng: it, him, them (pronoun) / of, 's (possessive) / to go to
之.notes: Most versatile particle. Here in 學而時習之 it is the object pronoun for what has been learned.

不.notes: Standard negation. Pronounced bú before 4th tone.

亦.notes: Literary particle. 不亦...乎 = 'is it not also...?'

說.notes: Read yuè here (pleasure), not shuō (to speak). Early variant of 悅.

乎.notes: Main rhetorical question marker. 不亦...乎 = 'is it not...?'

有.eng: to have, there is, to exist
有.notes: Negated by 無, not 不.

朋.notes: Implies a like-minded companion, not merely an acquaintance.

自.notes: As preposition = from. As pronoun = oneself.

方.notes: 遠方 = distant place. As adverb: just now, only then.

樂.notes: Also read yuè (music). Here read lè.

人.notes: 有人不知 = there are others who do not know. 人 here = other people.

慍.notes: The emotion of silent resentment when unrecognised. The gentleman does not feel it.

君.notes: 君子 = the noble/moral person, the gentleman — a key Confucian concept.

子.notes: Honorific title: 孔子 (Confucius), 孟子 (Mencius). 君子 = the noble person.

道.eng: way, path, the Way, to speak
道.notes: Central concept of Taoism. As verb = to say, to lead.

可.notes: Modal verb. 道可道 = the way that can be spoken of.

非.notes: Copular negative. 非常道 = not the eternal Way.

常.notes: 非常 = not-eternal. In modern Chinese 非常 = very — a semantic shift.

名.notes: Philosophically loaded in the Laozi. Names and reality diverge.

無.eng: to not have, there is no, nothingness
無.notes: Key Taoist concept. 無為 = non-action.

天.notes: 天地 = the universe. Both moral order and physical sky.

地.notes: Paired with 天 in 天地 (heaven and earth).

始.notes: 天地之始 = the beginning of heaven and earth.

萬.notes: 萬物 = the ten thousand things = all of existence.

物.notes: 萬物 = all things in the world.

母.notes: 有名萬物之母 = Having names is the mother of the ten thousand things.

曰.notes: Literary verb for 'to say'. 子曰 = The Master said.

吾.notes: Formal first-person pronoun. 吾道 = my Way.

一.notes: 一以貫之 = threaded through by one single thing.

以.eng: by means of, with, in order to
以.notes: Introduces instrument or means. 一以貫之 = unified by one.

貫.eng: to thread through, to run through
貫.notes: 一以貫之 = to thread everything through with a single principle.

曾.notes: 曾子, personal name Can — disciple known for filial piety.

門.notes: 門人 = people of the gate = disciples.

何.notes: 何謂也 = what does this mean?

謂.eng: to mean, to say, to be called
謂.notes: 何謂也 = what does this mean?

也.notes: Ends declarative sentences. Part of the 者...也 pattern.

夫.notes: Sentence-initial particle. When meaning man/husband read fū.

忠.notes: Giving full effort. Paired with 恕 as the two aspects of Confucius's single thread.

恕.notes: Extending oneself to others. 己所不欲，勿施於人.

矣.notes: 而已矣 = and that is all. A strong closing formula.
